title: AI-Driven Tumor Detection in Histopathology
speaker: Prof. M. Baumgartner
description: How deep learning models identify malignant regions in whole-slide images, outperforming traditional screening pipelines.

title: Chest X-Ray Interpretation with Deep Learning
speaker: Dr. S. Haller
description: A hands-on look at FDA-cleared AI tools for pneumonia and pneumothorax detection, and their integration into clinical workflows.

title: Computational Pathology: From Slides to Scores
speaker: Dr. F. Wettstein
description: Quantitative biomarker extraction from H&E stained slides using convolutional neural networks — clinical implications and caveats.

title: Bias, Fairness & Accountability in Clinical AI
speaker: Prof. T. Rätsch
description: Real-world case studies of algorithmic bias in patient triage tools and what regulatory frameworks require from hospitals.

title: Polygenic Risk Scores in Clinical Practice
speaker: Dr. L. Sterchi
description: How genome-wide association studies feed into patient risk stratification models and what they can — and cannot — predict.

title: LLMs for Clinical Documentation
speaker: Dr. A. Jaggi
description: Ambient voice AI, automated discharge summaries, and the liability questions every clinician should understand.

title: Foundation Models in Medical Imaging
speaker: Prof. M. Reyes
description: SAM, BioViL-T, and the new wave of large vision models pre-trained on radiology data — what clinicians need to understand.

title: Federated Learning Across Hospital Networks
speaker: Dr. C. Müller
description: Privacy-preserving model training without centralising patient data — key concepts, trade-offs, and Swiss regulatory context.

title: Predicting Therapy Response with Multimodal AI
speaker: Dr. V. Koller
description: Combining imaging, genomics, and clinical records to predict chemotherapy response — current evidence and limitations.
